TextENGNew: Smoke was rising here and there from the town of Okinomiya in the distance, like the city was on fire.
The haze of it hung ominously over the whole town, visible all at once from where we stood.
The wind occasionally carried strange cries, shouts of rage, and other unidentifiable ominous sounds that echoed through the silent town.
"......It sounds like the town's dying scream."
changeReason: The original omits that it is the town itself producing the dying scream (街の断末魔); the new translation correctly attributes the sound to the town.

TextENGNew: "This madness isn't just in Hinamizawa, is it......"
"Does this mean... the virus has finally spread to Okinomiya......?"
Rika had remained behind in Hinamizawa of her own choosing.
We'd tried to stop her, saying it was too dangerous — but looking at this sight, perhaps Hinamizawa, where we knew our way around, was actually the better option.
Mion was injured, but for the moment she seemed to be holding on.
Even so, she'd had nothing but first aid. She needed to see a doctor.
The Irie Clinic had been ransacked by the rioters, was already empty, and Irie himself was nowhere to be found.
"Anyway, we have to find a doctor."
"I'm fine. It hurts, sure, but it's settled down quite a bit."
"No. If you don't get proper treatment, it could fester later."
"There's nothing for it but to search for a doctor."
"Hard to imagine any doctors are open for business in a situation like this."
"......Let's head for the Sonozaki Group office. My mother and the others there should be able to do something......"
"You think Shii-chan's there too?"
"With things like this, that's the safest place to be."
"......I just hope Mion-san's mother and the others are still keeping their heads......"
changeReason: The original vaguely says 'those people' while the Japanese specifies '魅音さんのお母さんたち' (Mion-san's mother and the others); the new correctly identifies who Satoko is referring to.

TextENGNew: "Anyway, let's take a look at the town. We can't stay up here forever."
"......Right. Is this disaster only happening here, or has it spread to the rest of Japan? ......There's no way to know, stuck in Hinamizawa."
"The thought of this happening all over the world...... I don't even want to think about it......"
"Let's go. ......It's all right, I can walk on my own."
Normally we'd ride bicycles to Okinomiya.
But it was easy to imagine that Okinomiya, just like Hinamizawa, would have rioters playing at being the neighborhood watch.
The whole virus commotion had originated in Hinamizawa, after all. To the people of Okinomiya, anyone coming from Hinamizawa would naturally be no different from an enemy.
We decided it would be too dangerous on any road where those sorts of people might be lying in wait, and resolved to head there on foot through back paths instead.
The routes known only to elderly foragers and the children of Hinamizawa who ran wild through the fields — those would be the safest.
changeReason: The original incorrectly splits the Japanese into three groups (gatherers, the elderly, and children) when the Japanese specifies two: elderly foragers (山菜取りの年寄り) and children who run through the fields.

TextENGNew: Keiichi and the others set out for Okinomiya......
Keiichi's parents had taken shelter in the Sonozaki family's secret underground tunnels.
There were some provisions stored there, so they should be safe for a while.
Still, they needed a place that was even safer, with better access to medical care and food.
Confirming whether or not such a haven even existed was the primary objective behind scouting Okinomiya......
